# LinkedIn Post — OpenAI Partners with Accenture, BCG, McKinsey, and Capgemini in Enterprise Push

# Status: Review before posting

---

Enterprise AI just moved from vendor-led to execution-led — and the consulting giants are now the distribution channel.

OpenAI announced multiyear partnerships with Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey. These firms will help OpenAI's enterprise customers define and implement AI strategy. OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar confirmed that enterprise already represents roughly 40% of OpenAI's revenue, with a target of 50% by year-end. Anthropic is running the same playbook, having signed deals with Deloitte and Accenture.

This is a signal every CTO and CEO should read carefully. When the model providers start partnering with the firms that already sit in your boardroom, the sales cycle for enterprise AI collapses. Strategy and implementation are merging into a single conversation — and that conversation will be shaped by whichever partner gets to the table first.

The deeper implication: AI is no longer being sold as a technology layer. It's being sold as a business transformation engagement. Companies that treat AI adoption as a procurement decision will find themselves outpaced by competitors who treat it as an operating model shift — with or without the Big Four in the room.

GYanTech AI works directly with executive teams to build AI strategies that don't depend on a single vendor's roadmap -> www.gyantechai.com

With OpenAI and Anthropic both routing enterprise through consulting firms, how are you ensuring your AI strategy serves your business objectives — not a vendor's growth targets?

## Fact-check notes
- OpenAI entered multiyear partnerships with Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey & Co. — CNBC, Feb 23, 2026
- OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar stated enterprises account for roughly 40% of OpenAI's business, with expectations to reach ~50% by end of year — CNBC, Feb 23, 2026
- Anthropic has also inked deals with consulting giants including Deloitte and Accenture — TechCrunch, Feb 23, 2026
